annotations.label: Real number system (complete ordered field)

anchor.snippet: The real number system is the unique complete ordered field

annotations.note: ℝ is a conditionally complete linearly ordered field, and CompleteField.lean proves any such field is uniquely isomorphic to ℝ.

annotations.label: Least upper bound property

anchor.section: Order properties of the real numbers
anchor.snippet: has a least upper bound that is also a real number

annotations.note: Every nonempty bounded-above set has a least upper bound sSup via isLUB_csSup, which ℝ satisfies as a conditionally complete lattice.

annotations.label: Reals as a metric space

anchor.section: Topological properties of the real numbers
anchor.snippet: the real numbers become the prototypical example of a metric space

annotations.note: ℝ carries the metric space instance Real.metricSpace with distance |x − y|.

annotations.label: Metric topology equals order topology

anchor.section: Topological properties of the real numbers
anchor.snippet: turns out to be identical to the standard topology induced by order

annotations.note: The `instance : OrderTopology ℝ` establishes that the order topology coincides with ℝ's metric-induced topology.

annotations.label: Sequence

anchor.snippet: A sequence is a function whose domain is a countable

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated `Sequence` type; sequences are modeled directly as functions `ℕ → α`.

annotations.label: Real-valued sequence

anchor.snippet: a real-valued sequence , here indexed by the natural numbers, is a map

annotations.note: A real-valued sequence is just a function `ℕ → ℝ`, with no separate named definition in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: a real-valued sequence , here indexed by the natural numbers, is a map

annotations.label: Convergent and divergent sequence

anchor.snippet: is said to be convergent ; otherwise it is divergent

annotations.note: Convergence is `Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 L)` and divergence its negation, via the general filter Tendsto.

anchors.snippet: is said to be convergent ; otherwise it is divergent

annotations.label: Bounded sequence

anchor.snippet: is bounded if there exists

annotations.note: A bounded sequence is one whose range satisfies `Bornology.IsBounded (Set.range u)`.

annotations.label: Monotonic sequence

anchor.snippet: is monotonically increasing or decreasing if

annotations.note: Monotone/Antitone capture monotonically increasing/decreasing sequences exactly.

anchors.snippet: is monotonically increasing or decreasing if

annotations.label: Subsequence

anchor.snippet: is a subsequence of

annotations.note: Subsequences are modeled as `u ∘ φ` for a strictly monotone extraction `φ : ℕ → ℕ`, with no standalone `Subsequence` definition.

annotations.label: Limit of a function (ε-δ)

anchor.snippet: be a real-valued function defined on

annotations.note: Function limits are `Tendsto`, with the ε-δ form available as Metric.tendsto_nhdsWithin_nhds.

anchors.snippet: be a real-valued function defined on

annotations.label: Convergence of a sequence

anchor.snippet: converges to

annotations.note: Sequence convergence is `Tendsto u atTop (𝓝 L)`, with the ε-N form Metric.tendsto_atTop.

annotations.label: Limit as the variable increases without bound

anchor.snippet: increases without bound

annotations.note: Limits as the argument increases without bound are Tendsto along the filter `Filter.atTop`.

annotations.label: Cauchy sequence

anchor.snippet: is a Cauchy sequence if

annotations.note: CauchySeq is the Mathlib definition of a Cauchy sequence, with the ε-form Metric.cauchySeq_iff.

annotations.label: Cauchy iff convergent (completeness of R)

anchor.snippet: a real-valued sequence is Cauchy if and only if it is convergent

annotations.note: ℝ is a CompleteSpace, so by cauchySeq_tendsto_of_complete a real sequence converges iff it is Cauchy.

annotations.label: Monotone sequence bounded iff convergent

anchor.snippet: the sequence is bounded if and only if it is convergent

annotations.note: tendsto_atTop_ciSup shows a monotone sequence bounded above converges (to its supremum), the substantive direction of the equivalence.

annotations.label: Pointwise convergence

anchor.section: Uniform and pointwise convergence for sequences of functions
anchor.snippet: pointwise convergence of functions

annotations.note: Pointwise convergence is convergence in the product topology, characterized by tendsto_pi_nhds (`∀ x, Tendsto (F · x) p (𝓝 (f x))`).

annotations.label: Uniform convergence

anchor.section: Uniform and pointwise convergence for sequences of functions
anchor.snippet: Uniform convergence requires members of the family of functions

annotations.note: TendstoUniformly (and TendstoUniformlyOn) is the Mathlib definition of uniform convergence.

annotations.label: Cantor ternary set is compact

anchor.snippet: the Cantor ternary set

annotations.note: The middle-thirds Cantor ternary set as a compact subset of ℝ is not defined in Mathlib (only abstract Cantor schemes/perfect sets are).

annotations.label: Compact (closed and bounded)

anchor.snippet: is compact if it is closed and bounded

annotations.note: Mathlib defines compactness via filters but proves it equals closed-and-bounded for proper metric spaces in isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem

anchor.snippet: is known as the Heine-Borel theorem

annotations.note: isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded states the Heine–Borel equivalence of compactness with closed-and-bounded in proper spaces such as ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Sequential compactness

anchor.snippet: has a convergent subsequence

annotations.note: IsSeqCompact captures sequential compactness, with equivalence to compactness via isCompact_iff_isSeqCompact.

annotations.label: Compactness via open covers

anchor.snippet: has a finite subcover

annotations.note: isCompact_iff_finite_subcover gives the open-cover/finite-subcover characterization of IsCompact.

annotations.label: Cauchy sequences converge in compact metric space

anchor.snippet: any Cauchy sequence in a compact metric space is convergent

annotations.note: IsCompact.isComplete shows compact sets are complete, so cauchySeq_tendsto_of_isComplete gives convergence of Cauchy sequences there.

annotations.label: Continuous image of a compact set is compact

anchor.snippet: the image of a compact metric space under a continuous map is also compact

annotations.note: IsCompact.image proves the continuous image of a compact set is compact.

annotations.label: Continuity on an interval (via limit)

anchor.snippet: is a non-degenerate interval, we say that

annotations.note: Continuity on a domain is ContinuousOn, defined via ContinuousWithinAt limits at each point.

annotations.label: Continuity on a general domain (ε-δ)

anchor.snippet: is an arbitrary subset of

annotations.note: ContinuousOn captures continuity on an arbitrary subset, with the ε-δ form given by Metric.continuousOn_iff.

annotations.label: Continuity for topological spaces

anchor.snippet: are topological spaces, we say that

annotations.note: Continuous is the topological definition (preimages of opens are open).

annotations.label: Uniform continuity

anchor.snippet: is a subset of the real numbers , we say a function

annotations.note: UniformContinuous (and UniformContinuousOn) define uniform continuity, with the ε-δ form Metric.uniformContinuous_iff.

annotations.label: Continuous on compact implies uniformly continuous

anchor.snippet: all continuous functions are uniformly continuous

annotations.note: This is the Heine–Cantor theorem, formalized as CompactSpace.uniformContinuous_of_continuous.

annotations.label: Continuous but not uniformly continuous function

anchor.snippet: that is continuous but not uniformly continuous

annotations.note: No named Mathlib declaration exhibits a specific continuous-but-not-uniformly-continuous function as a counterexample.

annotations.label: Absolute continuity

anchor.snippet: is said to be absolutely continuous on

annotations.note: AbsolutelyContinuousOnInterval formalizes absolute continuity of a function on an interval, with the ε-δ form in absolutelyContinuousOnInterval_iff.

annotations.label: Absolutely continuous implies continuous

anchor.snippet: Absolutely continuous functions are continuous

annotations.note: AbsolutelyContinuousOnInterval.uniformContinuousOn proves absolutely continuous functions are uniformly (hence) continuous.

annotations.label: Derivative / differentiability

anchor.snippet: is differentiable at

annotations.note: HasDerivAt/DifferentiableAt/deriv formalize the derivative and differentiability at a point.

annotations.label: Differentiable implies continuous

anchor.snippet: if it is differentiable there

annotations.note: DifferentiableAt.continuousAt (and HasDerivAt.continuousAt) prove differentiability implies continuity.

annotations.label: Differentiability classes Cᵏ

anchor.snippet: One can classify functions by their differentiability class

annotations.note: ContDiff 𝕜 n f classifies functions by differentiability class Cᵏ for n : ℕ.

annotations.label: Smooth functions

anchor.snippet: are known as the smooth functions

annotations.note: Smooth (C^∞) functions are `ContDiff 𝕜 ∞ f`, the infinity case of the differentiability-class predicate.

annotations.label: Analytic functions class

anchor.snippet: consists of all analytic functions

annotations.note: AnalyticOn (equivalently `ContDiff 𝕜 ω` via ContDiffOn.analyticOn) is the class C^ω of analytic functions.

annotations.label: Series

anchor.snippet: we can define an associated series as the formal mathematical object

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal 'series' object; series convergence is handled via HasSum/tsum (unordered sums) or limits of Finset partial sums.

annotations.label: Partial sums

anchor.snippet: The partial sums of a series

annotations.note: Partial sums are written directly as `∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, a i` with no dedicated named definition.

annotations.label: Convergent series

anchor.snippet: is said to be convergent if the sequence consisting of its partial sums

annotations.note: Summable/HasSum capture convergence of a series, though as an unordered sum rather than via the ordered partial-sum sequence.

annotations.label: Geometric series (convergent)

anchor.snippet: An example of a convergent series is a geometric series

annotations.note: hasSum_geometric_of_lt_one (and tsum_geometric_of_lt_one) gives convergence and value of the geometric series.

annotations.label: Harmonic series (divergent)

anchor.snippet: the harmonic series has been known since the Middle Ages to be a divergent series

annotations.note: not_summable_one_div_natCast proves the harmonic series ∑ 1/n is not summable (diverges).

annotations.label: Absolute convergence

anchor.snippet: is said to converge absolutely if

annotations.note: Absolute convergence is expressed as `Summable (fun n => ‖a n‖)` with no dedicated predicate of its own.

annotations.label: Absolute convergence implies convergence

anchor.snippet: absolute convergence of a series implies its convergence

annotations.note: Summable.of_norm proves that summability of the norms implies summability of the series.

annotations.label: Series converging non-absolutely

anchor.snippet: an example of a series that converges non-absolutely is

annotations.note: The alternating series test (Antitone.tendsto_alternating_series_of_tendsto_zero) plus harmonic divergence gives conditionally convergent series, but no single decl states the example.

annotations.label: Taylor series

anchor.snippet: The Taylor series of a real or complex-valued function

annotations.note: taylorWithin/taylorWithinEval formalize Taylor polynomials/series of a function on a set.

annotations.label: Maclaurin series

anchor.snippet: the series is also called a Maclaurin series

annotations.note: A Maclaurin series is the special case of taylorWithinEval expanded at the base point 0, with no separate definition.

annotations.label: Analytic function via Taylor series

anchor.snippet: then the function is analytic

annotations.note: AnalyticAt means the function is locally the sum of a convergent power series, i.e. equals its Taylor series near the point.

annotations.label: Fourier series

anchor.snippet: Fourier series decomposes periodic functions or periodic signals into the sum

annotations.note: fourierCoeff/fourierBasis formalize Fourier coefficients and the Fourier series expansion of functions on the additive circle.

annotations.label: Tagged partition

anchor.snippet: is a finite sequence

annotations.note: BoxIntegral.TaggedPrepartition formalizes tagged partitions (with IsPartition for full partitions) in the box-integral framework.

annotations.label: Riemann sum

anchor.snippet: we define the Riemann sum of

annotations.note: BoxIntegral.integralSum is the Riemann sum of f over a tagged partition.

annotations.label: Mesh of a partition

anchor.snippet: The mesh of such a tagged partition is the width of the largest sub-interval

annotations.note: Mathlib's BoxIntegral controls partitions with gauge functions and distortion rather than defining the mesh/norm of a partition.

annotations.label: Riemann integral

anchor.snippet: We say that the Riemann integral of

annotations.note: BoxIntegral.integral with IntegrationParams.Riemann is the Riemann integral as a limit of Riemann sums.

annotations.label: Darboux integrable

anchor.snippet: A function is Darboux integrable if the upper and lower Darboux sums

annotations.note: Mathlib has no upper/lower Darboux sum integral (Darboux.lean concerns the intermediate value property of derivatives, not integration).

annotations.label: Darboux iff Riemann integrable

anchor.snippet: a function is Darboux integrable if and only if it is Riemann integrable

annotations.note: With no Darboux integral defined, the equivalence of Darboux and Riemann integrability is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus

anchor.snippet: integration and differentiation are inverse operations in a certain sense

annotations.note: integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt is the (second) fundamental theorem of calculus, ∫ f' = f b − f a.

annotations.label: Measure

anchor.snippet: an abstraction of length, area, or volume

annotations.note: MeasureTheory.Measure formalizes measures as countably additive functions extending an outer measure.

annotations.label: Distributions (generalized functions)

anchor.snippet: are objects that generalize functions

annotations.note: Distribution (continuous linear functionals on compactly supported test functions) and TemperedDistribution formalize generalized functions.

annotations.label: Distributional derivative of locally integrable functions

anchor.snippet: any locally integrable function has a distributional derivative

annotations.note: Distributions always have derivatives (derivCLM) and suitable functions embed via toTemperedDistribution, but the exact statement for arbitrary locally integrable functions is not a single declaration.
